purpose: Controls how much resume content the AI should generate for each seniority level. Layout JSON controls visual structure; this file controls content depth, counts, skill breadth, and level-appropriate writing.

globalRules.contentSource: Use the JD and job analysis as the primary content source.
globalRules.adjacentSkills: The AI may add reasonable adjacent skills only when they are normal for the detected role and level. Do not add niche tools, domains, metrics, certifications, or named platforms unless supported by the JD.
globalRules.companyNames: AI should suggest real, well-known companies that fit the JD domain, role, and seniority level. Do not copy company names from layout reference resumes. A company mentioned in the JD is the target employer, not automatically a past work-experience employer.
globalRules.locations: Experience locations are generated by AI/rules and are separate from any target job location in the JD. Do not apply the target job location to all prior experience.
globalRules.dates: Use realistic placeholder date ranges by level. Do not use exact dates from reference resumes.
globalRules.yearsConsistency: Any years-of-experience claim in the summary must match the generated work experience date ranges and stay within the level's allowed totalYearsRange.
globalRules.projectAlignment: Projects must align with the generated work experience domain, company context, and JD responsibilities. Do not create generic unrelated projects. If projects are included, they should sound like work performed in or adjacent to the generated companies.
globalRules.environmentProgression: Each work experience entry must have its own environment line. The current/recent role should use the strongest JD-aligned stack. Middle roles should use core implementation tools. Older or junior roles should use a smaller foundational stack. Do not repeat the exact same environment across every job.
globalRules.noEmptySections: Do not leave rendered sections empty. If a level has projectCount 0, the projects array must be empty and the renderer may skip it later.
globalRules.noUnsupportedMetrics: Do not create percentages, dollar amounts, user counts, latency numbers, uptime claims, or compliance claims unless the JD includes them.
globalRules.minimumExperienceBulletsPerEntry: Every work experience entry must include at least 5 bullets.
globalRules.skillSectionPrinciple: Skills should read like a resume technical environment, not a school syllabus. Use specific languages, frameworks, databases, cloud tools, testing tools, DevOps tools, observability tools, security tools, and methodologies appropriate to the detected level.

intern.projectPolicy: Include 3 project-heavy entries. Projects may be academic, capstone, internship, or portfolio projects, but must map directly to the JD stack and responsibilities.

environmentProgression.current: Internship/project environment using JD stack plus student-appropriate tools.
environmentProgression.older: Not applicable unless a second project/experience is generated.

entry.projectPolicy: Include 2 projects. At least one should connect to work or internship context; the other may be a technical project if it fills a JD skill gap.

environmentProgression.current: Entry-level professional environment with JD stack, Git, testing, API/database tools, and basic deployment tools.
environmentProgression.older: Internship environment with smaller stack: language, framework basics, database, Git, testing, and collaboration tools.

writingStyle.tone: hands-on implementation, debugging, testing, team contribution

junior.projectPolicy: Include 1 focused project only if it strengthens JD alignment. It should connect to work experience or production-style implementation, not coursework.

environmentProgression.current: Junior role environment with core JD stack, API/database tools, testing, logging, source control, and light DevOps.
environmentProgression.older: Associate role environment with foundational implementation tools and fewer advanced platform components.

writingStyle.tone: growing ownership, implementation, production support, maintenance

midlevel.projectPolicy: Include exactly 2 projects, or 0 only if the selected layout explicitly omits projects. Projects should be aligned to the generated work experience, such as a banking loan workflow, healthcare claims workflow, telecom billing workflow, or similar domain-specific system implied by the JD.

environmentProgression.current: Mid-level current role gets the broadest stack: JD stack, backend framework, database, cloud/devops, testing, monitoring, and integration tools.
environmentProgression.middle: Middle role should use core backend/API/database stack with moderate DevOps and testing tools.
environmentProgression.older: Older junior role should use foundational implementation stack and avoid advanced architecture/platform tools.

writingStyle.tone: independent delivery, service ownership, testing, deployment, production support

senior.projectPolicy: Include exactly 2 projects unless the selected consulting-style layout omits projects. Projects should be senior-level systems aligned with generated work experience and JD domain, not generic side projects.

environmentProgression.current: Senior current role gets full JD-aligned enterprise stack: architecture, cloud, microservices, messaging, DevOps, security, testing, observability.
environmentProgression.middle: Middle prior role should show strong implementation stack but less architecture/ownership tooling than current role.
environmentProgression.older: Older role should show earlier-career implementation stack with fewer advanced cloud, streaming, and observability tools.

writingStyle.tone: architecture, ownership, scalability, security, mentoring, delivery, cross-functional leadership
